title: Workspace Invite Flow
goal: Allow workspace admins to invite a teammate by email and track pending invites from the members page.
summary: This spec defines the first-pass teammate invite flow for workspace admins, including duplicate invite handling and visible pending status.

in: Workspace admins can send a single invite from the members page.
Pending invites are visible in the members list.
Duplicate pending invites for the same email are blocked with clear feedback.
out: Editing a teammate role after the invite is accepted.
Changes to the downstream invite acceptance experience.

constraints: Reuse the existing email delivery service.
Do not require a new admin permission model.
Keep the flow compatible with the current members page layout.
assumptions: The invite acceptance link infrastructure is already available.

user_stories.i_want: to invite a teammate by email
user_stories.so_that: they can join the workspace without manual account setup
notes: The first release supports one email address per action.

scenarios.title: Admin sends a new invite
given: The user is a workspace admin.
The target email does not already have a pending invite.
when: The admin enters a valid email address.
The admin submits the invite form.
then: A pending invite is created for that email address.
The system queues an invite email.
The members page shows the invite with a pending status.

scenarios.title: Admin tries to send a duplicate pending invite
given: The user is a workspace admin.
A pending invite already exists for the target email address.
when: The admin submits the same email address again.
then: A second pending invite is not created.
The UI explains that an invite is already pending.

acceptance_criteria.statement: Submitting a valid new email from the members page creates exactly one pending invite and queues exactly one invite email.
After a successful invite, the members page shows the invited email with a pending status without requiring a page reload.
Submitting an email that already has a pending invite does not create another invite and returns actionable feedback.

test_cases.description: Invite service creates one pending invite record and rejects duplicate pending invites.
Members page updates to show the pending invite after a successful submission.
Admin submits a new teammate invite from the members page and sees the pending state.

open_questions: Should expired pending invites be surfaced differently from newly created pending invites?
